name: Lysine sensor (LysG + CRISPRa)
shortDescription: Detects L-lysine — the flagship amino-acid product of C. glutamicum. The native LysG transcriptional sensor (used in the classic pSenLys biosensor) gates a CRISPRa circuit driving a fluorescent reporter, for strain screening and process monitoring.

input.detects: Intracellular/extracellular L-lysine (and basic amino acids) for strain screening and fermentation monitoring
input.operatingRange: LysG sensor responds across mM lysine

sensing.sensorMechanism: LysG (LysR-family) activates the lysE promoter in response to L-lysine (and arginine/histidine).
sensing.signalFlow: L-lysine -> LysG activates PlysE -> sgRNA -> dCas9-activator amplifies the reporter (CRISPRa) -> fluorescence rises with lysine. Basis of FACS-based producer screening.

parts.name: LysG regulator
parts.source: Native C. glutamicum lysG (LysR family); Binder et al. 2012 (pSenLys)

parts.name: sgRNA
parts.source: designed against / upstream of the reporter promoter

parts.name: dCas9-ω activator
parts.source: Cleto et al. 2016 (CRISPRi/a in C. glutamicum)

output.result: Fluorescence scales with lysine — enabling single-cell producer screening.

performance.notes: The LysG/lysE amino-acid biosensor (pSenLys) is validated for FACS-based strain screening; the CRISPRa-amplified version is a design template. Also responds to arginine/histidine.

buildSteps.title: Design sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Target/position the sgRNA; check C. glutamicum off-targets.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: Sensor -> sgRNA; dCas9; reporter. Use the host's standard toolkit.

buildSteps.title: Transform C. glutamicum
buildSteps.description: Transform, select, and confirm low background.

buildSteps.title: Expose to sample
buildSteps.description: Incubate with the sample plus a lysine standard curve.

provenance.source: Design template combining the LysG/lysE lysine biosensor with C. glutamicum CRISPRa
partsSources: Binder et al. 2012, Genome Biol (LysG/pSenLys amino-acid biosensor)
Cleto et al. 2016 (CRISPRi in C. glutamicum)
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
